title: Scope
fullText: These rules govern proceedings in the courts of the State of South Carolina to the extent and with the exceptions stated in Rule 1101.
plainEnglishSummary: This rule states where the South Carolina Rules of Evidence apply.
practiceNotes: Use this as an anchor rule when the threshold fight is whether the rules apply at all.

title: Definition of Relevant Evidence
fullText: Relevant evidence means evidence having any tendency to make the existence of any fact that is of consequence to the determination of the action more probable or less probable than it would be without the evidence.
plainEnglishSummary: Evidence is relevant if it moves a material fact, even a little.
practiceNotes: This is a low bar. Most fights are really Rule 403 fights, not Rule 401 fights.

title: Excluding Relevant Evidence for Prejudice, Confusion, Waste of Time, or Other Reasons
fullText: Although relevant, evidence may be excluded if its probative value is substantially outweighed by the danger of unfair prejudice, confusion of the issues, or misleading the jury, or by considerations of undue delay, waste of time, or needless presentation of cumulative evidence.
plainEnglishSummary: Relevant evidence can still come out if the downside substantially outweighs the value.
practiceNotes: Frame the ruling as a balancing decision and identify the specific unfair prejudice, not just harm.

title: General Rule of Competency
fullText: Every person is competent to be a witness except as otherwise provided by statute or these rules.
plainEnglishSummary: Competency is presumed unless a statute or another rule says otherwise.
practiceNotes: Do not mix this up with the federal language about civil cases. South Carolina Rule 601 is shorter.
